results.title: Addressing Late Payment Practices within Food and Beverage Sector, and Measures to Support Small Suppliers
results.summary: The Government stated it lacks data on late payment practices between businesses, including in the food and beverage sector. For disputes involving amounts up to $30,000, suppliers can file civil claims with the Small Claims Tribunals. Alternatively, suppliers may seek recourse through mediation and adjudication services provided by bodies such as the Singapore Mediation Centre. No specific measures to protect small suppliers from late payments by larger buyers were announced.

results.title: Addressing Needs of Workers who Care for Elderly Parents and Young Children and Proposal for "Sandwich Generation Leave"
results.summary: The Government commissions studies like the Quality of Life Study and National Population Health Survey covering caregivers generally, but does not specifically assess working sandwiched caregivers. Consequently, the Ministry will not introduce a distinct "Sandwich Generation Leave" due to the impracticality of catering to diverse needs with various leave types. Instead, support is provided through progressively enhanced parental leave provisions, voluntary employer caregiving leave, and the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests to help employees manage work and caregiving responsibilities.

results.title: Addressing Shortage of Licensed Electrical Workers
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority (EMA) is addressing the shortage of Licensed Electric Workers (LEWs) by collaborating with higher learning institutions and industry partners to attract younger entrants and support mid-career workers. Current efforts include enhancing curricula, launching a Technical Engineer Diploma in Electrical Engineering (Clean Energy) with the Institute of Technical Education, and introducing an Internship Training Plan to clarify work experience requirements for licensure. EMA is also upgrading preparatory courses to help skilled workers without formal academic qualifications qualify for a license. With approximately 3,700 LEWs in Singapore, 1,400 of whom are aged 60 and above, EMA will closely monitor supply and demand to develop additional measures to boost the workforce if necessary.

results.title: Ambulance Accessibility Issues at Private Condominiums and Emergency Response Mitigation Measures
results.summary: SCDF ambulances require a minimum 3.0-metre clearance height, but many private basement carparks are limited to 2.2 metres. In such instances, crews request alternative access routes via management or security, with timely patient access achieved in most cases. A joint advisory issued in March 2024 by SCDF and the Building and Construction Authority mandates raising clearance heights to 3.0 metres for new developments. The Minister confirmed these mitigation measures ensure emergency services reach distressed persons promptly despite existing infrastructure constraints.

results.title: Assessing Adequacy of Heavy Vehicle Parking Lots
results.summary: Currently, approximately 40,000 Heavy Vehicle (HV) lots exist, with 32,000 HVs registered under the Vehicle Parking Certificate (VPC) scheme requiring designated overnight parking. While private lot holders retain discretion to allocate spaces for operational needs, Government agencies will factor in the availability of private lots open for public enquiry when assessing islandwide adequacy. This assessment will be conducted in collaboration with industry stakeholders like the Singapore Transport Association to better address parking needs.

results.title: Assessment of Developments in Myanmar
results.summary: Singapore assesses Myanmar's situation as dire five years post-coup, characterized by widespread conflict, instability, heavy casualties, and mass displacements between the Tatmadaw, Ethnic Armed Organisations, and People's Defence Forces. Control over many areas remains contested, hindering recovery from the 28 March 2025 earthquake. The Minister stated that durable reconciliation requires constructive dialogue involving all stakeholders, with immediate priorities being violence de-escalation, humanitarian access, and creating conditions for dialogue. For upcoming elections in late-2025 or early-2026, peace, security, and inclusive participation must underpin credible outcomes reflecting the people's will. Singapore will continue supporting ASEAN and the ASEAN Chair in implementing the Five-Point Consensus, emphasizing that solutions must be Myanmar-owned and Myanmar-led.

results.title: Assessments to Determine Adequacy of Childcare Capacity within Pasir Ris-Changi GRC
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) monitors childcare demand in Pasir Ris-Changi GRC, which falls under the Pasir Ris Planning Area, by analysing population size, demographics, and enrolment trends. ECDA collaborates with the Housing Development Board and Urban Redevelopment Authority to develop new preschools alongside new housing or within private developments. As of September 2025, the Pasir Ris Planning Area hosts 45 childcare centres offering approximately 400 infant care places and 5,490 childcare places. These places remain partially unsubscribed, with about 65% of infant care and 70% of childcare places currently occupied. ECDA also facilitates capacity expansion through atypical sites like co-located integrated developments and by retrofitting disused State properties where necessary.

results.title: Assistance Given to Caregivers of Persons with Dementia
results.summary: The Government enhances long-term care subsidies for dementia day care and the Home Caregiving Grant in 2026 to support caregivers facing financial and psychological strain. The Agency for Integrated Care (AIC) deploys seven CREST-PDS teams by December 2024 to provide timely, free education and resource coordination within six months of diagnosis, while CREST-CSN offers mental well-being support. Caregivers can access free services via AIC Link offices, hotlines, or the digital Care Services Recommender. For respite, options include dementia day care at 130+ senior care centres, nursing home respite for up to a month annually, and enhanced Home Personal Care Services (HPC+). The Government monitors healthcare expenditure sustainability while advancing preventive health measures to manage dementia risk factors like high blood pressure.

results.title: Awareness of Community Relations Unit in Handling Neighbour Disputes and Preventing Noise-related Disputes through New BTO Housing Design
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development addressed queries regarding neighbour dispute escalation to the Community Relations Unit (CRU) and resident awareness of resolution pathways in a reply dated 14 October 2025. The Minister also confirmed plans to implement upstream engineering interventions in new Build-To-Order (BTO) housing designs to mitigate inter-floor noise. Additionally, the response compared current construction materials against global standards for noise and insulation. Specific details on the reasons for non-escalation and the exact nature of the engineering interventions were not provided in this transcript excerpt.

results.title: Balancing Academic Rigour Required to Excel at PSLE With Skills to Thrive in AI-driven Economy
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Primary School Leaving Examination (PSLE) assesses core concepts and critical thinking rather than rote learning or test-taking skills. To prepare students for an AI-driven economy, schools integrate inquiry-based, collaborative, and experiential learning pedagogies across all subjects and grade levels through co-curricular and applied learning programmes. These 21st Century Competencies, including collaboration and inventive thinking, are developed continuously within the curriculum and are not solely measured by a single exam. While international benchmarking studies confirm Singaporean students' adeptness in reasoning and solving complex problems, the Ministry regularly compares its practices with other education systems through a robust regional and international community of practice to ensure teaching methodologies meet individual student needs.

results.title: Balancing Employability Preparation with Youths' Well-being and Preventing Excessive Internship Stacking
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that internships are not compulsory for secondary or post-secondary students, with autonomous university students averaging two internships while polytechnic and ITE students average one over three years. While specific guidelines on internship stacking numbers are deemed impractical due to diverse industry needs, the Ministry encourages students to prioritise meaningful experiences over quantity. Schools and Institutes of Higher Learning remain committed to supporting student well-being, offering programmes like career fairs to help explore career pathways without mandating excessive work placements.

results.title: Breakdown of Total Number of Car Ownership by Housing Type as of 2025
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport provided a breakdown of household car ownership by property type as of 2025 in Table 1. The response details the absolute number of HDB versus non-HDB households owning at least one, two, or three or more cars. Specific figures for each category are contained within the referenced table. No further commentary or analysis was offered regarding the data trends or implications.

results.title: Addressing Teachers' Stress Levels and Supporting Their Mental Well-being
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) addresses high teacher stress and workload, noting an average of 47 hours per week while maintaining stable hours through holistic reforms. Recruitment has been ramped up to over 1,000 annually to replace retirees and fill gaps, with non-teaching duties shifted to allied educators to protect core instructional time. Recent workload audits and clear guidelines now help itemise tasks across classroom, administrative, and pedagogical domains, supported by a survey response rate exceeding 90% to ensure accurate data capture. The government acknowledges that while teaching is a human endeavour, unsustainable workloads must be avoided, with specific measures including flexible work arrangements and professional development to support teacher well-being and retention.

results.title: Breakdown of Updates on Companies Investing in Johor-Singapore SEZ by Industry Type and Revenue Size
results.summary: Since the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) agreement was signed in January 2025, the JS-SEZ Project Office has received over 300 enquiries from Singapore and foreign multinational corporations. These interests span manufacturing, logistics, and the digital economy, with small and medium enterprises accounting for nearly half of all submissions. The Minister did not provide specific figures for the total number of interested companies, a breakdown by industry type, or the count of companies with annual revenue under $100 million.

results.title: Calculation and Source of Funding for Subsidy to Cover Costs from Deferred 9.4% Increase in Public Transport Fares
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the estimated $200 million additional subsidy for 2026 covers the deferred 9.4% public transport fare adjustment. This quantum was derived from projected 2026 revenue and the specific deferred fare amount. The funding will be drawn from the Consolidated Fund. The response provided these specific figures and sources without detailing the full derivation methodology or long-term fiscal impact.

results.title: CareShield Life Statistics and Severe Disability Prevention Measures
results.summary: As of June 2025, CareShield Life has 2,179 active claimants, with the majority (44%) aged 30 to 59, reflecting the scheme's 2020 introduction and optional status for those born in 1979 or earlier. Stroke and accidents causing fractures are primary causes of sudden severe disability, while chronic conditions like hypertension and diabetes, alongside progressive illnesses such as dementia, lead to gradual disability onset. To mitigate these risks, the government employs preventive strategies including Healthier SG to prevent chronic diseases and Age Well SG to manage frailty and delay long-term care needs among seniors. These national initiatives aim to support healthy ageing and reduce the incidence of severe disability across different age and vulnerability groups.

results.title: Cases where MDW's Medical Fees Exceeded $60,000 and Proposal to Enhance Coverage Sums in MDW Insurance Policies
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, 99% of Migrant Domestic Worker (MDW) inpatient and day surgery bills in public healthcare institutions remained within the $60,000 minimum annual claim limit. Consequently, the Ministry of Manpower has no plans to raise this limit further, as only approximately 20 bills annually exceed the threshold. However, from 1 July 2025, exclusion clauses will be standardised to provide employers with greater clarity on coverage. Employers seeking enhanced protection beyond the standard policy are encouraged to purchase more comprehensive insurance plans to secure additional peace of mind.

results.title: Collecting Sector-specific Data on Employee Stress, Burnout, Anxiety and Depression
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower currently derives insights into workplace mental health through anonymised, aggregated data from the voluntary iWorkHealth assessment tool. To enhance sector-specific and occupational-level analysis, MOM is collaborating with tripartite partners to encourage broader iWorkHealth adoption and is exploring joint initiatives with the Ministry of Health to improve data collection mechanisms. These efforts aim to better inform targeted prevention and intervention strategies for employee stress, burnout, anxiety, and depression across different industries.

results.title: Collection of Data on Lonely Deaths for Social Support Intervention Planning for Vulnerable Individuals
results.summary: The Ministry of Health stated it does not collect data on residents dying alone in their homes. Instead, the Ministry focuses on preventing such deaths through effective outreach and befriending initiatives. These efforts rely on collaboration among community partners, including Silver Generation Ambassadors, Active Ageing Centre volunteers, and grassroots advisers. The Minister emphasized that organizing these community resources is essential to supporting seniors, particularly those living alone.

results.title: Companies that Have Retrenched Employees Without Mandatory Retrenchment Notification of Authorities
results.summary: In September 2025, it was reported that Agoda retrenched employees in early August 2025 while complying with the Mandatory Retrenchment Notification (MRN) requirement for employers with 10 or more staff. The Ministry clarified that submitting an MRN within five working days after informing affected employees is sufficient, as advance notification is not mandated. This approach balances employers' need to finalize decisions with the prompt provision of career facilitation services. The National Trades Union Congress has proposed considering advance notification requirements, though no specific actions were taken against non-compliance in the reported case.

results.title: Comparison of Public Playground Numbers and by Facility Types over Past 10 Years
results.summary: Currently, approximately 2,800 public playgrounds are operated by the Housing Development Board and National Parks Board within HDB estates and parks. The Minister noted that the total number of playgrounds has increased over the years compared to the past decade. Specific breakdowns for facilities with swings or sand play areas, as well as precise figures from 10 years ago, were not provided in the response.

results.title: Complaints of Employers Failing to Pay Workers Minimum Salaries Stipulated by Progressive Wage Model
results.summary: In the first six months of 2025, the Ministry of Manpower received fewer than 20 substantiated complaints regarding employers failing to pay Progressive Wage Model minimum salaries, a figure comparable to the same period in 2024. The Ministry continues to raise PWM awareness and encourages employees to verify wages via the Progressive Wage Portal. Workers detecting discrepancies are advised to seek clarification from employers or approach MOM for assistance.

results.title: Complaints of Unsatisfactory or Disputed Insurance Claims
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) enforces fair dealing guidelines requiring insurers to promptly pay legitimate claims and maintain sound internal processes. Consumers must first contact insurers for complaints; unresolved disputes can be escalated to the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre (FIDReC) for low-cost mediation or adjudication. Over the last three years, FIDReC resolved an average of 246 annual insurance claim disputes, while MAS received 91 similar complaints yearly, representing less than 0.01% of total claims. More than 85% of FIDReC cases were resolved without litigation, and less than 0.1% of claims from larger insurers proceeded to court. MAS may take action against non-compliant insurers, including policy reviews and consumer restitution.

results.title: Consideration of Attendance at Drug Rehabilitation Centre during Fit and Proper Assessment for Career in Financial Services Industry
results.summary: Admission to the Drug Rehabilitation Centre does not automatically disqualify an individual from a career in Singapore's financial services industry. Under the Monetary Authority of Singapore's "Guidelines on Fit and Proper Criteria," hiring decisions for individuals with past records must consider the seriousness of conduct, time elapsed, and assumed roles. Financial institutions retain the discretion to apply additional selection requirements, including experience, academic qualifications, and skillsets, when evaluating candidates for specific positions.

results.title: Considerations for Converting HDB Car Park Space for Alternative Use
results.summary: HDB evaluates requests to convert multi-storey car parks (MSCPs) for alternative uses like sports or gardens based on parking demand, resident impact, and safety constraints including fire provisions and structural loading. While exceptions for low-utilisation estates are not explicitly granted, HDB supports conversions where technically feasible and beneficial to the community. Requests are assessed case-by-case, with past conversions limited to upper levels for greenery. No blanket relaxation of requirements is stated; all proposals undergo site-specific technical assessment before approval.

results.title: Considerations when Allocating Counsellors to Schools
results.summary: School counsellor allocation relies on student caseloads and indicators like social-emotional well-being monitored via termly check-ins, which are independent of counsellor access levels. Educators' mental health needs are addressed through dedicated programmes separate from school counselling services and do not influence allocation decisions. When increased needs are detected, schools can augment capacity by engaging flexi-adjunct counsellors. Beyond schools, the Ministry leverages whole-of-Government resources including Response teams, CHAT, CREST-Youth, and helplines like mindline 1771. Students are educated on these support avenues through the Character and Citizenship curriculum.

results.title: Contributing Factors to Rise in Malnutrition among Older Singaporeans
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while social isolation and low health literacy are risk factors, malnutrition rates among older adults have remained stable since 2019, with roughly one in four aged 50–69 failing to meet minimum caloric needs and one in two missing protein recommendations. The Ministry addresses these issues through national programmes like Healthier SG and Age Well SG, which combine community education, such as the "Steady Lah" interactive activities, with primary care interventions to identify at-risk seniors. Financial support is provided via the Community Development Council Voucher Scheme for daily expenses and the Meals-on-Wheels service for vulnerable individuals unable to prepare meals. Additionally, the Health Promotion Board collaborates with supermarkets to increase affordable healthy protein options and partners with food businesses to make nutritious meals accessible across various budgets and settings.

results.title: Cost Effectiveness of Electrical Fire Prevention Measures in HDB Estates
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that Singapore's fire safety regulations are benchmarked against international standards and best practices while explicitly considering cost-effectiveness. The Ministry will consider the Member's suggestion to commission a study evaluating costs saved from averted property damage, healthcare expenses, and emergency response costs in HDB estates. This potential study aims to better inform the cost-effectiveness and justification for future regulatory policies regarding electrical fire prevention measures.

results.title: Criteria for Inclusion in Chronic Disease Management Programme and Reasons for Non-inclusion of Eczema
results.summary: The Clinical Advisory Committee (CAC) has established specific criteria for Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) inclusion, which were detailed in response to a written question on 25 September 2025. Assessment of eczema-related conditions for CDMP inclusion is currently underway, indicating that eczema has not yet been included in the programme. The Minister for Health confirmed that the evaluation process is active but did not specify the final timeline for eczema's potential inclusion or the precise reasons for its current exclusion beyond the ongoing assessment status.

results.title: Current Training Certification towards Lifestyle Medicine Competencies for Healthcare Professionals
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that promoting healthy lifestyle habits, including nutritious eating and regular physical activity, is a core learning outcome for healthcare students in medical and nursing programmes. No specific training or certification pathways for lifestyle medicine competencies were identified as currently existing or under development. The response indicates these principles are integrated into general curricula rather than through dedicated certification schemes. Further details on emerging pathways were not provided in the transcript.

results.title: Curriculum Time Spent on PSLE Preparation
results.summary: The Ministry of Education does not track actual curriculum time spent on PSLE preparation, including past paper study, by subject. Instead, MOE provides guidance on syllabus completion time to ensure appropriate pacing and revision opportunities. Since the removal of mid-year examinations, schools have utilized the freed-up time for engaging learning experiences and strengthening 21st Century Competencies. Schools plan revision schedules based on student needs while avoiding excessive exam preparation, aligning with efforts to reduce over-emphasis on examination grades. MOE continues to guide curriculum time usage while allowing local adjustments to meet student needs.

results.title: Data on "Cut and Fill" Approach to Reassign COE Quotas and Further Medium-term Plans to Equalise Supply
results.summary: Since May 2023, the Land Transport Authority has redistributed and injected a total of 30,813 Certificates of Entitlement (COEs) across all categories using a "cut-and-fill" approach to reassign quotas from guaranteed deregistrations of five-year non-extendable and standard 10-year COEs. The Ministry stated it will continue monitoring COE supply to ensure stability. The Acting Minister for Transport did not disclose specific figures for each COE category or confirm any further medium-term plans to equalise supply and eliminate price cycles beyond the stated monitoring commitment.

results.title: Data on Current Healthcare Subsidy Schemes for Special Needs Children and Identifying Potential Subsidy Coverage Gaps
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track the specific number of special needs children benefiting from healthcare subsidy schemes. Subsidies are allocated based on patient medical needs and household income tiers rather than specific condition tracking. Consequently, there are no stated plans to study or identify coverage gaps for this group in the current response. The Minister clarified that assistance is distributed generally to lower-income families without distinguishing special needs status in the data.

results.title: Data on Employers who Voluntarily Adopt National Wages Council 2024/2025 Guidelines
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower tracks employer adoption of the National Wages Council 2024/2025 Guidelines through the Survey on Annual Wage Changes. The adoption rate for lower-wage workers (earning less than $2,500 monthly) will be published in the upcoming annual report on "Wage Practices for Lower-wage Employees and Forward Expectations of Establishments." Specific percentages for the 5.5% wage increase threshold and comparative data from previous years were not provided in the response.

results.title: Data on Firearm Violations at Police and Auxiliary Police Units for Past 24 Months and Subsequent Enforcement Actions
results.summary: In the past 24 months, one Police Officer and three Auxiliary Police Officers faced firearm handling violations; investigations continue for the officer while the three APOs have been charged. The Ministry of Home Affairs enforces strict controls including mandatory security vetting, annual competency certification, and barring unfit personnel from carrying firearms. Firearms are stored in secure armouries with rigorous issuance and return processes that verify officer identity, authorization, and timely return. Periodic audits and spot checks further ensure adherence to standard operating procedures. These measures collectively manage risks associated with firearm assignment and return within police and auxiliary units.

results.title: Data on Household Income Distribution of Students Admitted through DSA Scheme and who Participated in Private DSA Preparatory Programmes over Past Five Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) does not track household income distribution or participation in private preparatory programmes for students admitted via the Direct School Admission (DSA) scheme. Consequently, no data exists to correlate DSA benefits with early talent identification versus socioeconomic advantage. The Minister clarified that private preparatory programmes are not required for DSA consideration, as primary schools utilize co-curricular activities, arts, music, PE, and Applied Learning Programmes to develop student talents. Secondary schools apply a holistic selection approach focusing on commitment, strengths, potential, and personal qualities rather than past achievements. MOE continues to refine the scheme to ensure accessibility for students from all backgrounds, including through the Junior Sports Academy for those with sporting potential.

results.title: Data on Individuals Adjudicated Bankrupt by Age Group and Repeat Bankruptcies in Past 10 Years
results.summary: From 2016 to September 2025, the Minister for Law provided tabulated data on individuals adjudicated bankrupt across four age bands: 24 and below, 25 to 39, 40 to 54, and 55 and above. The response also included counts of repeat bankruptcies within each respective age group. Specific numerical values and percentages for these categories are contained in the referenced table. No further policy measures or legislative changes were discussed in this exchange.

results.title: Data on Out-of-Pocket Spending for Chronic Diseases by CHAS Card Tier and Income Decile and Plans for Further Subsidies
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not possess readily available data on the distribution of annual out-of-pocket spending for CHAS cardholders with chronic diseases by card tier and income decile. While 14% of the 2.7 million cardholders utilised CHAS chronic subsidies in 2024, the remaining majority either do not require chronic disease management or manage care through other settings and payment arrangements like employee medical benefits. Consequently, specific figures on utilisation rates, average unused balances, and the proportion of common chronic medications on the Standard Drug List were not provided. MOH reviews subsidies regularly to ensure adequacy amid rising healthcare costs, noting that most common chronic medications are already subsidised under the Standard Drug List and Medication Assistance Fund List.

results.title: Data on Outcomes of Bankruptcy Applications and Mandating Mediation between Petitioning Creditor and Bankruptcy Respondent
results.summary: For 2016 to 2022, 3,741 bankruptcy applications (17% of total) were withdrawn by petitioning creditors, while 8,455 applications (39%) did not result in bankruptcy orders; data on terminations for want of prosecution is not captured. The Ministry of Law does not mandate mediation between petitioning creditors and respondents, noting that the existing Debt Repayment Scheme already includes a structured creditor meeting to review proposed repayment plans before approval. Any new requirement to mandate mediation would require careful study to ensure cost-efficient administration. The Ministry will continue reviewing the bankruptcy regime to balance debtor and creditor interests without introducing unassessed procedural changes.

results.title: Data on Scam Mule Investigations from January to June 2025 and Interim Support Measures for Unknowing Victims
results.summary: The Minister directed the Member to refer to the response to Question No 97, delivered on 15 October 2025, for details on scam mule investigations from January to June 2025. This prior response covers the number of individuals who unknowingly transferred funds or lost account control, as well as interim support measures for those restricted from banking services under the facilities restriction framework. The current parliamentary record does not contain the specific statistics or support details requested, as they were provided in the earlier written answer.

results.title: Data on Singaporean Victims Baited by Scam Syndicates to Work Overseas
results.summary: Since 2022, the Government has provided consular assistance to nine Singaporeans potentially exploited by overseas scam centres, with three accepting aid for return and travel document issuance. In all instances, insufficient evidence existed to conclude the individuals were held captive or exploited by syndicates. No specific data was reported regarding the total number of cases involving potential victims baited to work overseas.

results.title: Data on Suicides and Attempted Suicides among Seniors above 60
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health reported that annual suicide deaths among seniors aged 60 and above have generally declined over the past five years, with male deaths approximately double female deaths. Disaggregated data by race and housing type is unavailable, and no comprehensive statistics exist for suicide attempts. Identified risk factors include relationship breakdowns, mental health conditions, and prior suicide attempts. Prevention efforts rely on the national mindline 1771 and SOS Hotline 1767 for 24/7 crisis support. The Silver Generation Office under the Agency for Integrated Care proactively identifies vulnerable seniors and connects them to community mental health teams for assessment and counselling. Those facing loneliness are referred to Active Ageing Centres for befriending services and active ageing programmes. A related written question by Dr Choo Pei Ling is scheduled for a future Sitting.

results.title: Data on Temporary Permit Exhibition Scheme Permits Issued to Firms Lacking Local Employees in Past Two Years
results.summary: Over the past two years, 1,280 Temporary Permit Exhibition Scheme (TPES) permits were issued to firms lacking local employees or CPF-registered staff, representing 10% of total TPES permits issued in that period. The Minister for Manpower confirmed these figures, noting that such firms have received permits despite having no local workforce or registered staff. This data highlights the proportion of exhibition permits granted to entities without domestic staffing requirements during the specified timeframe.

results.title: Data to Support Move towards Skills-based Hiring in Civil Service
results.summary: The Public Service has adopted a competency-based framework to guide recruitment, requiring agencies to specify relevant skills and competencies in advertisements while clearly indicating if specific qualifications are mandatory for specialised roles like accountancy or engineering. Recruiters assess all candidates holistically based on skills, experience, and alignment with job requirements, regardless of whether formal academic requirements are listed, potentially using assessments like case studies. The government does not track the percentage of job postings waiving academic qualifications or employing skills-based assessments as the primary criterion. No specific targets or timelines for accelerating skills-based hiring adoption were stated.

results.title: Dealers Bidding up COE Premiums while Keeping Car Prices Stable
results.summary: LTA reports that vehicle sale prices have risen alongside higher Certificate of Entitlement (COE) premiums, though not proportionally, driven by factors like lower Open Market Values and electric car subsidies. The COE clearing price is set at the highest unsuccessful bid plus $1. Currently, buyers may bid for new COEs directly or delegate this to dealers for convenience. The Ministry states there is no evidence of dealers artificially inflating COE premiums beyond buyer willingness to pay.

results.title: Decision and Disbursement Duration of Withdrawal Applications for CPF Reduced Life Expectancy Scheme
results.summary: The CPF Board reviews Reduced Life Expectancy Scheme applications within 14 working days after receiving the completed Medical Assessment Report, though complex cases requiring medical clarification may take longer. Upon approval, savings are disbursed to the member's bank account within three working days. Applications from members certified with terminal illnesses or advanced-stage medical conditions are currently expedited. The Board is committed to processing these applications efficiently to ensure timely financial support while continuously reviewing its processes.

results.title: Delayed or Missing CPF Contributions when Platform Workers Switch Platforms
results.summary: Since the Platform Workers Act enactment, CPF monitoring has identified administrative errors causing incorrect contributions, which were promptly resolved. While the Ministry acknowledges feedback regarding delayed contributions for up to two months when workers join new platforms, it maintains that the current 15th-month cut-off date is legislated under the CPF Act to balance operational feasibility with accurate contribution rates. Consequently, the Ministry has declined to mandate immediate first-month contributions or require back payments, citing potential cash-flow strain on workers with inconsistent earnings. The Ministry is currently engaging platform operators to assess whether existing arrangements can be made more efficient while maintaining practical administrative workability.

results.title: Developing Age-progressive Framework for Responsible Use of GenAI for Students and Parents across Educational Levels
results.summary: The Ministry of Education has addressed the development of an age-progressive framework for generative AI by providing schools with guidance on age-appropriate use within the Guide to Digital Literacy and Technological Skills. Additionally, parents can access resources such as "A Parent's Guide to Generative AI tools for Learning" via the Parents Gateway to support younger students' home learning. These measures cover primary, secondary, and post-secondary levels, with specific guidance already published for schools and institutes of higher learning as of 24 September 2025.

results.title: Developing Framework for Continuous Professional Development in Brain-based Teaching Approaches for NIE Educators
results.summary: The Ministry of Education and the National Institute of Education (NIE) confirm that brain-based learning principles, including cognitive load management and spaced retrieval, are already integrated into both pre-service teacher preparation and in-service professional development courses. The Science of Learning in Education Centre at NIE actively conducts neuroscience research to translate these insights into effective pedagogical practices. Consequently, the Ministry and NIE will continue collaborating to ensure teachers apply developmentally appropriate pedagogies informed by neuroscience and educational psychology, addressing the query regarding existing frameworks without announcing new specific measures.

results.title: Differentiating Validity Periods of HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) System and Enhancing HFE Letter with Personalised Information and Resources
results.summary: There are no plans to introduce differentiated validity periods for the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter, which remains valid for nine months to balance buyer convenience with assessment currency. While the letter currently provides three personalised loan scenarios and integrates with e-services like "Calculate Housing Budget," the Minister confirmed no recalibration mechanisms will be added to accommodate changing incomes or market conditions. To assist young couples with unstable employment, the Deferred Income Assessment scheme allows applicants to secure a flat before finalising their income assessment for grants and loans. Buyers are directed to utilise existing planning tools for a holistic understanding of their housing and financing options.

results.title: Diplomatic Relations with Syria and Opportunities for Trade, Reconstruction and Humanitarian Engagement
results.summary: Singapore maintains diplomatic relations with Syria and has engaged the Syrian Transitional Government, confirmed by a meeting with Syrian Foreign Minister Asaad Hassan Al Shaibani in New York in September 2025. The Ministry is open to considering cooperation with Syria in areas where Singapore can provide assistance, though specific opportunities for trade, reconstruction, or humanitarian engagement were not detailed. No timeline or eligibility criteria for such cooperation were stated in the response.

results.title: Duration of Free Morning Off-peak Rail Rides Scheme on North East Line
results.summary: The Government is piloting a fully funded free morning off-peak rail ride scheme on the North East Line (NEL) and Sengkang-Punggol LRT to reduce peak-hour crowding and improve commuter experience. The Acting Minister for Transport stated the pilot will run for one year, after which the scheme's extension will be evaluated based on its take-up rate and effectiveness in shifting approximately 7% of commuters off-peak. The programme is not contingent on increasing NEL peak-hour capacity, as technical limitations regarding underground station lengths prevent extending train carriages. While the Seletar Line is under study for potential acceleration, the current focus remains on short-term adjustments like this pilot and adding new buses to manage demand alongside long-term infrastructure planning.

results.title: Criminal Law (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill
results.summary: The Government introduced mandatory caning for scammers and syndicate recruiters while making caning discretionary for enablers like SIM card providers and payment account holders under the Criminal Law (Miscellaneous Amendments) Bill. This legislative update recalibrates penalties across 17 statutes to enhance deterrence against scams, address misuse of technology for abusive materials, and protect public servants from doxxing. The Ministry clarified that the new Protection from Online Falsehoods and Manipulation Act offence does not require proof of diminished public confidence, and legitimate feedback remains protected if based on mistaken facts. Additionally, the Bill implements 2019 Children and Young Persons Act changes to ensure reformative training and imprisonment remain available for older youth offenders, while reviewing caning age limits in light of increasing life expectancy.

results.title: Easement of Anti-Scam Measures by Banks in "Whitelist" and/or Considerable Safe Situations
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has implemented safeguards for bank accounts with balances of at least $50,000 that are rapidly drained, delaying further transfers to allow consumers time to verify legitimacy. This measure excludes whitelisted transactions, specifically recurring standing instructions, recurring GIRO/eGIRO payments, and bill payments to classified billing organisations including Government agencies. MAS and banks are monitoring implementation to minimise disruptions while protecting against scams, alongside other initiatives like MoneyLock and the phasing out of SMS One Time Passwords. Consumers are encouraged to plan ahead for large value transfers, acknowledging that some inconvenience is necessary to prevent fraud.

results.title: Effectiveness of Covert versus Overt Cameras in Preventing Speeding Offences
results.summary: The Ministry for Home Affairs states that overt and covert cameras serve distinct purposes rather than being interchangeable. Overt cameras provide specific deterrence in high-risk accident areas, while covert cameras offer general deterrence to reinforce safe driving behaviour. Consequently, the Ministry considers research comparing their relative effectiveness on speeding offences as not useful, as the two systems address different operational objectives within the traffic enforcement framework.

results.title: Effectiveness of Shared Stay-in Senior Care Services Sandbox and Other Enhanced Caregiving Support Measures
results.summary: The Shared Stay-in Senior Caregiving Services sandbox, launched in 2023, aims to enable seniors to age in the community by offering alternative caregiving options. Positive feedback from providers and families indicates the model effectively supports seniors living alone, provided they possess cognitive ability or have a next-of-kin for decision-making. Due to this success, the service will be mainstreamed by end-2025. Concurrently, new operators will receive work pass flexibilities, including additional foreign worker quotas and source relaxations, with further details to be shared before the end of 2025.

results.title: Effectiveness of SkillsFuture-funded Programmes on Job-related Outcomes and Expanding List of Approved Certifications on MySkillsFuture Portal
results.summary: The Ministry of Education and SkillsFuture Singapore track programme effectiveness using administrative data, surveys, and econometric studies, with results published on the MySkillsFuture portal. Singaporeans may use their SkillsFuture Credit base tier to offset fees for examination-only certifications, provided the qualifications are recognised by relevant sector agencies. Currently, approximately 360 such certifications are listed on the portal. International certifications from bodies based overseas or not registered with SkillsFuture Singapore are excluded from the approved list. Individuals holding relevant international qualifications may write to SkillsFuture Singapore to request reimbursement for examination fees using their base tier credit.

results.title: Efforts at Minimising Practice of Roads being Resurfaced or Re-excavated for Different Works
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) enforces a one-year "no-opening" moratorium on newly resurfaced roads to prevent abortive works, though emergency utility requests may trigger flexible re-excavation at the full cost of the respective utility service providers with no additional taxpayer burden. While the Road Opening Coordination Committee (ROCC) facilitates coordination rather than directing works, it promotes solutions like Common Services Tunnels (CSTs) for high-load areas and Utilities Specific Ducts (USDs) for HDB estates to consolidate infrastructure access. CSTs are deemed unsuitable for new HDB towns or major redevelopment zones due to cost and site-specific constraints, whereas USDs offer a more appropriate alternative for residential estates.

results.title: Eligibility Assessment of Participants under SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance Scheme
results.summary: Under the SkillsFuture Mid-Career Training Allowance scheme, the allowance is computed using the latest 12 months of earned income data from Government records, including Central Provident Fund contributions and Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore records. Consequently, income from short-term or part-time work reflected in these records is taken into account when determining the allowance. This applies to all participants, including those who have been retrenched or are undergoing career transition, ensuring that any recorded earnings within the specified period affect the final computation.

results.title: Employer Compliance Rates for Local Qualifying Salary and Progressive Wage Requirements
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower reported that approximately 80% of eligible employees received salaries meeting Progressive Wage Model and Occupational Progressive Wage requirements. For workers not covered by these models, 90% earned at least the Local Qualifying Salary. These compliance rates reflect employer adherence to wage standards across different categories within the past year. No specific timelines or future targets were stated in the response.

results.title: Employer Data Submission for Compliance Monitoring of Progressive Wage Model and Local Qualifying Salary Requirements
results.summary: As of June 2025, over 90% of employers have submitted required data to the Ministry's Occupational Employment Dataset for compliance monitoring. This submission covers assessments for the Progressive Wage Model and Local Qualifying Salary requirements. The Minister for Manpower confirmed this high submission rate, indicating strong adherence to the specified wage frameworks. No further details on non-compliant employers or specific penalties were provided in the statement.

results.title: Enforcing Employment of Foreign Manpower Act 1990 to Curb Illegal Freelance Work in Creative Sector
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) enforces the Employment of Foreign Manpower Act 1990 by monitoring illegal freelancing in the creative sector through tip-offs and complaints. MOM requests removal of social media advertisements for suspected foreign freelancers and collaborates with the Visual, Audio, Creative Content Professionals Association (Singapore) to educate stakeholders on work pass requirements. Additionally, MOM partners with the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority to share intelligence on suspected illegal freelancers and deny their entry into Singapore.

results.title: Enhanced Regulatory Measures for Private Food Delivery Companies on Safety and Traffic Compliance of Delivery Personnel
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) and Traffic Police (TP) enforce existing traffic laws and penalties on all road users, including private food delivery personnel. Mandatory requirements include theory test certification for Power-Assisted Bicycles and e-scooters, as well as the use of front and rear lights during darkness on public paths and roads. Platform companies are responsible for rider safety and must ensure adequate third-party liability insurance coverage for riders using active mobility devices on public paths. In June 2025, LTA partnered with industry associations to launch an e-guide emphasizing safe riding and parking practices. Additionally, campaigns like "Be Safe, Be Seen" and safety talks with Grab and foodpanda promote safer habits. No new mandatory training or specific penalties for repeated breaches were introduced in this response.

results.title: Enhancing Accountability Measures for Errant Assisted Mobility Device Users Involved in Fire Incidents
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that importing, selling, using, or keeping non-compliant Power-Assisted Bicycles (PABs) and motorised Personal Mobility Devices (PMDs) is an offence enforced by the Land Transport Authority (LTA), with non-compliant devices immediately seized. While criminal liability for fires caused by rash or negligent acts depends on case-specific facts under the Penal Code, current common law already allows affected residents and insurers to seek full financial compensation through civil proceedings for damages resulting from negligent conduct. The Minister stated that existing laws can hold errant device users personally liable, and the Ministry will continue enforcing these rules while engaging users on electrical safety and safe device usage.

results.title: Enrolling Persons below 40 with Multiple Chronic Health Conditions in Healthier SG Programme
results.summary: The Ministry will not allow residents under 40 to enrol in Healthier SG, as the programme targets those aged 40 and above who are more susceptible to co-morbidities. Instead, younger Singaporeans with chronic diseases receive care through regular primary care follow-ups. They may access Community Health Assist Scheme subsidies and MediSave for chronic condition treatment under the Chronic Disease Management Programme. No mechanism for exceptional cases to receive integrated care support was proposed.

results.title: Ensuring Materials from Unlicensed Financial Advisors Still Accessible Online No Longer Carry Risk of Misleading Consumers
results.summary: Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) issued advisory letters to five content creators for offering unlicensed one-on-one financial coaching, noting that such practices risk breaching regulations if they constitute tailored advice or specific product recommendations. The creators have since deleted relevant content and adjusted practices, though MAS identities remain unpublished as no breaches were established. New guidelines for content creators and financial institutions regarding digital advertising will formally take effect in March 2026, with financial institutions expected to implement them progressively in the interim. MAS retains the authority to take enforcement action against unlicensed financial advice providers even before the new guidelines become effective.

results.title: Establishing Collaboration between NIE and ECDA for Brain-based Learning across Learning Profiles and Integrating Adaptive AI in Education System
results.summary: Ongoing collaborations between the National Institute of Education, Early Childhood Development Agency, and other partners already integrate neuroscience and educational psychology into curriculum design. The Ministry grounds its pedagogies in these research fields and incorporates expert views as appropriate. No new interdisciplinary taskforce or specific roadmap for integrating brain-based learning and adaptive AI was proposed or established during this session. Existing research partnerships continue to inform educational theories and practices across the system.

results.title: Expanding Learning Support Programmes to Include all Primary School Subjects and Levels
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that current learning support programmes target students with greater needs by strengthening literacy and numeracy foundations through specific initiatives like the Learning Support Programme, Reading Remediation Programme, and Learning Support for Mathematics. These programmes are delivered from Primary 1 to Primary 6, with recent expansions extending Learning Support for Mathematics to Primary 3 and 4 students since 2023, and enhancing Reading Remediation for Primary 3 to 5 students this year. While the Ministry does not plan to expand support to all subjects and levels as a blanket policy, schools and teachers provide targeted subject-specific support as needed. The Minister noted that academically weaker students generally perform better than international peers in English, Mathematics, and Science, indicating the effectiveness of existing customised support.

results.title: Expanding SkillsFuture Credit Eligibility for Subscription to AI Productivity Tools
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that SkillsFuture Credit currently covers AI-related courses, which may include access to premium AI tools within a structured learning environment. This approach facilitates hands-on learning by applying practical tools to real-world problems. The Ministry will continue monitoring the rapidly evolving AI landscape to support workers and enterprises in harnessing AI effectively. No immediate expansion of eligibility specifically for standalone subscriptions to AI productivity tools was announced.

results.title: Expanding Uses of CDA Funds and Applying Means-testing to CDA Top-ups for More Equitable Assistance to Lower-income Households
results.summary: From May this year, CDA fund usage expanded to cover all preschool incidental charges, including optional in-school enrichment programmes, aligning with Edusave provisions. The Ministry declined to extend CDA funds to out-of-preschool enrichment activities to safeguard resources for core preschool fees and incidental charges. Regarding top-ups, they remain a broad-based measure rather than means-tested; lower-income households instead access additional preschool subsidies or seek financial assistance from Social Service Offices. No further expansion of CDA permissible uses or means-testing for top-ups was proposed.

results.title: Expansion Plans on Intergenerational Co-living Models for Next Three to Five Years
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health clarified that intergenerational co-living models, such as Commune @ Henderson, serve as one housing option rather than the primary solution for Singapore's ageing population. While these models increase variety, the Ministry will maintain a spectrum of other housing-care options including Community Care Apartments, Shared Stay-in Senior Caregiving models, Nursing Homes, and Age Well Neighbourhoods to cater to diverse senior needs. Specific expansion plans, target numbers for sites and residents, and future site selection criteria for the next three to five years were not stated in the response.

results.title: Expansion of Target Screening and Counselling for Women Aged 40 to 50 in Primary Care and Community Settings
results.summary: Existing screening and counselling pathways for women aged 40–59 and menopause management are already integrated into the healthcare framework under Healthier SG. Primary care doctors are trained to assess menopausal symptoms and recommend management, while public resources and groups like "Dynamite Daisies" provide additional support. The Minister clarified that no expansion of these pathways is proposed, as current mechanisms encourage women to inform their doctors for assessment and recommendations. Outcomes by age and income band were not stated as a planned publication.

results.title: Expediting Review of Penalty Framework against Sellers and Users of Non-compliant Personal Mobility Devices
results.summary: The Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority (LTA) are considering tougher enforcement actions against the sale, use, and keeping of non-compliant Active Mobility Devices, including strengthening regulations on online sales. Significant penalties are currently imposed on offenders for illegal modifications or possession of such devices. The Ministry targets to make necessary legislative amendments next year to address the issue. Regular operations continue to enforce against non-compliant devices on public paths and against retailers selling them.

results.title: Extending Concession Pass Price Reductions beyond Adults, Seniors and PwDs in view of Transport Fare Increase in December 2025
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that concession pass prices are determined during each Fare Review Exercise, where adjustments are cross-subsidised across commuter groups. The Public Transport Council (PTC) recently lowered monthly pass prices for students and full-time national servicemen by up to 10% in 2023, noting that pre-university student passes remain the lowest priced. Consequently, the Ministry will not extend the December 2025 fare increase concessions to these groups beyond the existing reductions, as their current rates already reflect the latest review outcomes.

results.title: Extending Expiry Date of Unused $500 SkillsFuture Credits
results.summary: The Ministry of Education will address the proposal to extend the expiry date of unused $500 SkillsFuture credits by six months, specifically for futureproofing courses in artificial intelligence, information technology, communications, and healthcare. This response will be provided alongside other related Parliamentary Questions at the next available opportunity. The current expiry date for these credits remains the end of 2025 until a formal decision is announced. No immediate extension has been confirmed in this session.

results.title: Feeder Bus Service for Compassvale Crescent Residents
results.summary: LTA has confirmed there is no change to its assessment regarding a feeder bus service for Compassvale Crescent residents. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that similar inquiries raised on 12 November 2024 and 4 February 2025 were already addressed, and the current position remains unchanged. Consequently, residents facing a walk of over 400 metres to the nearest bus stop will not receive a new dedicated feeder service at this time.

results.title: Findings from Crane Incident at HDB Worksite in Sin Ming on 10 October 2025
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) cannot yet share preliminary findings on the 10 October 2025 crane incident at Sin Ming due to ongoing investigations. Under existing Workplace Safety and Health regulations, mobile crane dismantling must be performed by approved contractors following manufacturer instructions or under an authorised examiner's supervision. Employers are directed to refer to Approved Codes of Practice for safe lifting guidance. MOM will review current safety requirements once investigation findings are available to strengthen protections for workers in lifting and dismantling operations.

results.title: Follow-up Actions to Rectify Restrictions for Persons Acquitted as Scam Mules
results.summary: The Minister referred Members to the response to Question No 97, delivered on 15 October 2025, regarding restrictions on bank accounts for persons acquitted as scam mules. No new measures, timelines, or specific steps to lift these restrictions were stated in this session. The transcript does not provide details on recourse for livelihood harm or the current status of the facility restriction framework for acquitted individuals. Further information is available in the referenced prior response.

results.title: Foreign Participation in Singapore's Commercial and Industrial Property Markets over Past Five Years
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, foreign buyers accounted for less than 20% of private commercial and industrial property transactions in Singapore. The Minister for National Development stated that landlords, whether local or foreign, generally price rents competitively to attract tenants and avoid vacancy costs. Consequently, foreign participation is not the main driver of commercial and industrial rent movements over this period. The Ministry assesses that foreign ownership patterns have not significantly influenced overall rent trends.

results.title: Full-time NSFs Declaring Psychological or Mental Health Conditions Prior to Enlistment and Support Provided
results.summary: The Singapore Armed Forces reported that 9% of pre-enlistees and 11% of full-time National Servicemen (NSFs) sought mental health support in 2024, reflecting a general upward trend consistent with national youth statistics. Pre-enlistment medical examinations already include mental health screening conducted by in-house psychiatrists, with individuals posing risks to themselves or others deferred until conditions improve. Those cleared to serve receive additional monitoring, while the SAF implements psychological first aid training for recruits and commanders to support peer assistance. The Home Team maintains a similar support system, and the SAF continues collaborating with families and relevant agencies to ensure servicemen's well-being.

results.title: Governance, Regulatory and Commercial Factors to Address to Enable Smooth and Reliable Wind Power Imports under Vietnam-Malaysia-Singapore Agreement
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry stated that the Vietnam-Malaysia-Singapore offshore wind power project remains in an early development stage. Consequently, the Ministry is unable to comment on specific governance, regulatory, or commercial hurdles, nor can it provide timelines or prospects for wind-power imports at this time. No definitive measures or constraints regarding the project's smooth and reliable implementation have been disclosed in the parliamentary response.

results.title: Groups Exempted from Caning for Vaping-related Trafficking Offences
results.summary: Under the Misuse of Drugs Act, caning applies to vaping-related trafficking only when controlled drugs are involved; mere importation or sale of e-vaporisers without drugs does not attract caning. Men over 50 and all women are legally exempt from caning under section 325 of the Criminal Procedure Code (CPC), while juveniles sentenced to imprisonment remain liable unless specific CPC provisions apply. Although criminal syndicates may exploit exempt groups, the CPC allows courts to impose up to 12 months of additional imprisonment for exempt offenders to ensure sufficient punishment and deterrence.

results.title: Impact of HDB Resale Prices on Process of Valuing Land for BTO Flats
results.summary: The Chief Valuer determines fair market value for HDB land purchases using established principles that reference resale transactions alongside site-specific attributes. The Minister clarified that land cost paid by HDB is distinct from the final BTO flat price, which is set based on affordability rather than cost. Consequently, no specific discount or multiplier is applied to resale comparables for Standard, Plus, or Prime projects; these categories are not conflated regarding land valuation. The valuation process remains independent and follows professional standards applicable to other valuations.

results.title: Impact of NUS' Five-day Work-from-office Policy on Talent Acquisition
results.summary: The Ministry of Education noted that NUS retains autonomy over workplace arrangements and has received staff feedback regarding its five-day work-from-office policy, prompting a review for appropriate support. NUS confirmed it continues to offer flexible work arrangements for specific roles, acknowledging that talent attraction depends on multiple factors rather than a single policy. The Minister stated there is no one-size-fits-all approach, as organisations must balance operational needs with staff requirements when adopting workplace policies.

results.title: Implications of China’s Export Curb of Rare Earth Element Materials on Singapore's Trade and Technology Sectors
results.summary: China's recent export controls on rare earth elements are not bans but licence-based measures intended to promote legitimate trade, with the government assuring prudent implementation. The impact on Singapore companies has been minimal so far, though potential delays and increased compliance risks exist for firms dependent on Chinese supply chains for affected raw materials. The Ministry encourages all companies to account for these regulations in their international business activities to minimise disruptions.

results.title: Improving Work-life Balance and Mental Well-being of Educators in View of OECD Teaching and Learning International Survey 2024 Findings
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed that questions regarding teachers' work-life balance, mental health impacts of extended hours, and potential protected rest-time pilots be addressed in the oral reply to Questions Nos 2 to 5 on 4 November 2025. This response covers assessments of TALIS 2024 findings showing Singapore teachers work 47.3 hours weekly with higher stress than the OECD average, as well as how well-being data informs staffing and retention policies. No new measures were announced during this session; all inquiries were deferred to the scheduled oral reply.

results.title: Incorporating Animal Welfare and Anti-cruelty Themes and Modules in School Syllabus
results.summary: Animal welfare and anti-cruelty themes are currently integrated into Character and Citizenship Education, Social Studies (Primary), and Science, with contexts regularly refreshed to include contemporary examples. These lessons are accessible via the Student Learning Space for independent review. The Ministry will continue reviewing curriculum content as part of its ongoing curriculum review and refresh process. No specific plans to expand these modules into exam questions or designate them as voluntary learning modules were confirmed in this response.

results.title: Increasing Intake for Educational Psychology Master's Programmes to Support Needs and Development of Neurodiverse Children
results.summary: The National Institute of Education (NIE) currently offers the sole Master of Arts (Applied Psychology) programme training Educational Psychologists (EPs) in Singapore's autonomous universities. The Ministry of Education (MOE) confirmed that NIE can increase intake numbers if additional demand arises. MOE regularly reviews EP deployment and training needs to ensure sufficient support for schools, where EPs collaborate with Special Educational Needs Officers and teachers to assist students with special educational needs. The Ministry continually monitors system capacity to address evolving student and school requirements.

results.title: Investigations into Child Trafficking and Illegal Adoptions in Last Five Years Resulting in Prosecutions and Convictions
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Singapore Police Force investigated 59 alleged child sex trafficking cases under the Prevention of Human Trafficking Act, Penal Code, Women's Charter, and Children and Young Persons Act, with no reported cases of child labour trafficking or trafficking under the Adoption of Children Act 2022. Of the 59 cases, 34 proceeded to prosecution, resulting in 27 convictions while seven remain under or pending court proceedings. The remaining 25 cases were either still under investigation, concluded with stern warnings or composition fines, or involved no further action against initial suspects. No specific statistics were provided for illegal adoptions, as no cases were reported under the relevant adoption legislation during this period.

results.title: Learning Points from Discontinued Marine Parade Shuttle Bus Trial
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that learning points from the discontinued Marine Parade shuttle bus trial will inform future assessments of Community Development Council (CDC) funds for transport offerings. The pilot, primarily funded by Grassroots Organisation donations with a $200,000 seed grant from the South East CDC, revealed variable route usage patterns based on resident needs. Consequently, bus frequencies were adjusted and one route was discontinued earlier this year after data analysis. The Minister stated that Grassroots Organisations will continue piloting and adapting resource deployment to better serve connectivity needs, with Marine Parade GRC Advisers deciding to terminate the service to deploy resources more targetedly. Further details on the reallocation of funds are expected to be announced soon.

results.title: Lessons from Overseas Counterparts for Google's Implementation of Age Assurance Measures
results.summary: The Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI) has engaged international counterparts including the USA, UK, EU, and Australia to cross-share best practices on age assurance. Under the Code of Practice for Online Safety for App Distribution Services, designated app stores such as Google Play, Apple App Store, and others must implement these measures by 31 March 2026. Additionally, MDDI will begin engaging six designated social media services, including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and X, on similar age assurance requirements later this year.

results.title: Lessons from Recent Spate of Gastroenteritis Outbreaks at Preschools
results.summary: Investigations into recent gastroenteritis cases at preschools are ongoing while the Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) and Singapore Food Agency issue a joint advisory urging operators to exercise vigilance. Preschools must conduct daily health checks, follow infectious disease management guidelines, notify ECDA of suspected cases, and adhere to hygiene requirements regarding ventilation, cleaning, and food safety. To enforce these standards, ECDA conducts unannounced licensing visits to assess compliance, taking regulatory actions such as warnings or financial penalties against non-compliant operators. The agency continues monitoring the situation to ensure the well-being of children and staff.

results.title: Lift Installation for Pedestrian Overhead Bridges across CTE and PIE Connecting Whampoa to Neighbouring Towns to Enhance Accessibility for Seniors
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will install lifts at four pedestrian overhead bridges connecting Whampoa to adjacent towns across the Pan Island Expressway and Central Expressway, prioritising these over existing ramps. As of October 2025, the bridge between Jalan Tenteram and Kim Keat Avenue has been upgraded, while site studies have commenced for bridges at Jalan Rajah/Toa Payoh Stadium and Whampoa Drive/Hong Wen School. The Minister confirmed these specific locations are being addressed to improve accessibility for seniors, though a definitive completion date for all four sites was not stated in the response.

results.title: Long-term Support and Compensation Available for Teachers Injured or Disabled during Classroom Incidents
results.summary: Teachers injured or disabled during classroom incidents can choose coverage under the Work Injury Compensation Act (WICA) or Civil Service work injury provisions for medical expenses. Regardless of the chosen scheme, eligible teachers receive up to one year of paid injury leave under WICA, with additional yearly medical leave available for further absence. For permanent incapacity, lump sum compensation under WICA ranges from $116,000 to $346,000 for accidents occurring on or after 1 November 2025. Partial permanent incapacity compensation is prorated based on income and age. Teachers may also utilise yearly medical benefits to cover treatments exceeding WICA claim limits or duration.

results.title: Long-term Sustainability of Annual Public Transport Subsidies, Impacts on Future Fare-setting and Transparency Safeguards in Subsidy Allocation
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the Public Transport Council (PTC) independently determines annual fare adjustments using a formula tracking cost drivers and economic conditions. For the current review, the PTC approved a 5% fare increase, deferring 9.4% of the allowable 14.4% quantum. To cover this gap, the Government will provide an additional $200 million subsidy in the coming year, calculated by the Land Transport Authority based on projected 2026 revenue and actual rail ridership. This specific amount is distinct from the over $2 billion in annual subsidies required to sustain bus and train operations, ensure service reliability, and fund asset renewal. Safeguards for transparency in subsidy allocation across operators remain part of the broader framework governing these distinct funding streams.

results.title: Managing Usage, Hogging and Cost of EV Charging Stations at Public Car Parks
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that charger utilisation rates are monitored closely, with plans to scale up deployment at high-demand locations subject to electrical capacity. To deter lot hogging, operators have implemented idle fees at several charging sites. Charging prices are determined by operators based on demand, location, and power rating, rather than a government-led cost review. The government will collaborate with operators to address growing demand where feasible.

results.title: Mandating Employers to Notify MOM Prior to Retrenchment Exercises
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower is reviewing a proposal from the National Union Congress to mandate advance retrenchment notifications before informing affected employees, rather than the current requirement to submit notices within five days of notification. This change would apply to specific scenarios under consideration by tripartite partners. The Ministry will release further details on the proposal's status and potential implementation timeline when appropriate.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Singaporeans without Permanent Addresses Are Not Excluded from Government Support Schemes
results.summary: Eligible Singaporeans temporarily without a fixed residential address are not excluded from Government schemes; eligibility is determined by the NRIC address rather than current residence. Households residing at non-residential addresses or sharing a registered address with others may claim vouchers by visiting any community centre or club with supporting documentation, such as a tenancy agreement, to prove separate household status. Under the enhanced Climate Friendly Households Programme, each place of residence receives one set of Climate Vouchers. Singaporeans who have rented out their properties are ineligible for vouchers for those specific properties but remain entitled to claim vouchers at their current place of residence.

results.title: Media Coverage of Large Lottery Wins and Managing Problem Gambling
results.summary: The Ministry of Digital Development and Information states that news organisations exercise editorial discretion to ensure reporting remains responsible, accurate, and compliant with applicable content codes, which prohibit normalising or encouraging gambling on linear television and radio. The Gambling Regulatory Authority (GRA) regulates gambling advertisements by requiring Singapore Pools to seek approval for every ad, ensuring content does not excessively induce gambling or mislead on winning probabilities. For TOTO draws, GRA permits Singapore Pools to publish jackpot sizes, winner counts, and ticket purchase locations on its website to maintain transparency. Additionally, the Ministry of Social and Family Development enforces social safeguards such as prohibiting betting on credit, while the National Council on Problem Gambling conducts regular public education campaigns during peak periods like Chinese New Year. GRA and MSF will continue monitoring the situation and reviewing safeguards as necessary.

results.title: Mental Health Assessments for Police, Police NSFs and Auxiliary Police Officers Handling Firearms
results.summary: Police Full-time NSFs, Auxiliary Police Officers, and regular officers undergo psychological assessments during recruitment and enlistment, with most APOs from specific agencies also assessed. Those authorised to carry firearms must pass annual shooting tests and undergo continuous suitability evaluations, where supervisors monitor well-being and immediate redeployment occurs if psychological distress is suspected. The Singapore Police Force provides a Psychological Fitness Training Framework covering resilience and stress management, alongside in-house psychological services, peer support programmes via para-counsellors, a 24/7 helpline, and external agency-administered services. Auxiliary Police Officers receive similar support through mental wellness briefings, employee assistance programmes, and supervisor networks. Regular campaigns, workshops, and pulse surveys further promote mental health awareness and allow officers to raise workplace concerns.

results.title: Mental and Emotional Well-being Support for Educators in Special Education Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that the Ministry of Education (MOE) collaborates with Social Service Agencies (SSAs) to support the mental and emotional well-being of Special Education (SPED) teachers. Initiatives include establishing two SPED Communities of Practice, recruiting staff at seven job fairs, and exploring common digital solutions to reduce administrative workload. Schools also maintain dedicated staff well-being committees to provide practical stress management support. These measures aim to foster a sustainable work environment and retain educators within the sector. The Minister noted that previous initiatives were shared in September 2024, with current updates focusing on peer support, professional collaboration, and workload sustainability.

results.title: Monitoring of Cases and Actions Taken against Errant Insurance Companies Preventing Legitimate Claims
results.summary: The Government expects insurers to promptly pay legitimate claims and adhere to Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) Guidelines on Fair Dealing and Risk Management Practices. MAS will take appropriate action against errant insurers, which may include requiring policy reviews and restitution to consumers. Consumers should first contact insurers for complaints; unresolved cases can be escalated to the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre (FIDReC) for mediation or adjudication. Over the last three years, FIDReC resolved an average of 246 annual insurance disputes, while MAS received 91 similar complaints yearly, representing less than 0.01% of total claims. Litigation involving larger direct insurers accounts for less than 0.1% of their received claims.

results.title: NEL Morning Peak Hour Commuter Boarding Patterns from Punggol Coast to Kovan MRT for CBD versus non-CBD Destinations
results.summary: Acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow stated that approximately one-third of weekday morning peak hour commuters boarding the North-East Line between Punggol Coast and Kovan alight within the Central Area. The remaining two-thirds disembark at stations outside the Central Area. This statistic applies specifically to the segment from Punggol Coast to Kovan during weekday morning peak hours. No further breakdown or timeline for future adjustments was provided in the response.
