results.title: Need for Clarification on Statements made by the Leader of the Opposition
results.summary: On 21 October 2025, Mr Pritam Singh clarified that his earlier description of Mr Noor Deros as having "gate-crashed" a meeting on 20 April 2025 was inaccurate, as Mr Deros was invited by an ustaz who informed Mr Faisal Manap one hour before the event. Mr Singh admitted he was unaware of this invitation until 21 October, explaining that his use of "gate-crashed" reflected his knowledge at the time that no WP candidate had specifically sought Mr Deros out. He further clarified that while Mr Faisal Manap knew of Mr Deros from prior events, other Malay/Muslim candidates did not know him personally. The clarification corrected the parliamentary record regarding the nature of Mr Deros's attendance and the basis for the initial statements made during the General Elections and the Ministerial Statement on Race and Religion.

results.title: New Initiatives for Support and Outreach to Senior Caregivers with Dependents
results.summary: The Government will implement higher subsidies for long-term care, adult disability services, and caregiving respite services next year. Additionally, the Home Caregiving Grant quantum will be raised to better support those caring for persons with moderate disabilities at home. The Silver Generation Office will continue outreach to single senior caregivers living alone, connecting them to community resources like Active Ageing Centres for befriending activities and necessary support. These measures aim to ease the burden on caregivers through enhanced financial assistance and expanded community access.

results.title: Notifying Families of Death Row Prisoners on Executions and Permitting of Final Visits or Calls
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that Datchinamurthy Kataiah's execution was scheduled for 25 September 2025. His family received advance notification of the date and was permitted daily visits, including an extended period at each session. On the evening of 24 September 2025, the Singapore Prison Service informed the family that clemency petitions submitted to the President had been lodged. The President subsequently rejected these petitions on 25 September 2025, and the execution proceeded in the afternoon as directed by the Court. The Minister stated that the Singapore Prison Service follows established procedures to notify families and manage final visits before carrying out the sentence.

results.title: Number of Appeal Cases Annually Since Commencement of CareShield Life and Follow-up Actions for Improvements
results.summary: Since CareShield Life launched in 2020, three appeal cases have been received, with zero successful outcomes. All appeals are reviewed by independent panels of healthcare professionals. To improve the assessment process and applicant convenience, the Ministry of Health and Agency for Integrated Care are expanding house call assessors at Public Health Institutions and streamlining claims applications across disability schemes. No further lessons or specific timeline for process changes were detailed in the response.

results.title: Number of Dementia-afflicted Patients Over Five-Year Period and Future Projections for Singapore
results.summary: In 2023, approximately 74,000 persons in Singapore were living with dementia, according to the Institute of Mental Health's second Well-being of the Singapore Elderly (WISE) Study. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health projects this figure will rise to 152,000 by 2030 as the nation ages. This estimate reflects the anticipated increase in dementia prevalence driven by demographic shifts toward a "super-aged" society.

results.title: Number of Finance, Accounting, Human Resources and IT Positions Outsourced or Relocated Overseas
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower does not collect data on the number of finance, accounting, human resources, or IT positions outsourced, relocated overseas, or converted to contract arrangements over the past three years. Despite this data gap, resident employment has grown in IT, financial services, and legal, accounting, and management sectors. To safeguard job security and progression for locals amid business restructurings, the Government offers initiatives including the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme, Career Conversion Programmes, and the Mid-Career Pathways Programme to help Singaporeans upskill and pursue new roles. Additionally, the Overseas Markets Immersion Programme supports employers in developing overseas career opportunities for local professionals. The Government continues to review measures under the Economic Strategy Review to ensure locals can adapt to accelerating economic changes.

results.title: Number of Non-compliant Noise Levels at HDB BTO Construction Sites in Fernvale between August to October 2025
results.summary: Between August and October 2025, the National Environment Agency (NEA) detected one instance of non-compliance with regulatory noise limits at the Fernvale Sails Build-To-Order site in Fernvale, while 14 noise feedback reports were received from both Fernvale Sails and Fernvale Oasis projects. The contractor responsible for the Fernvale Sails violation was fined. NEA continues to monitor these sites and will enforce penalties against any future breaches of noise regulations.

results.title: Outcomes of Efforts to Preserve Orderliness in and around Waterloo Mall Given Illegal Hawking
results.summary: In 2020, the Singapore Land Authority (SLA), Singapore Food Agency (SFA), and Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) conducted a street enhancement exercise at Waterloo Mall to improve public health, safety, and streetscape. As part of this initiative, SLA issued Temporary Occupation Licences (TOLs) to existing street vendors, requiring them to operate within designated spaces. Agencies regularly conduct joint operations, supported by the Singapore Police Force (SPF), to enforce licensing conditions and maintain order, particularly during peak periods. For unlicensed hawkers with complex challenges such as social isolation or medical conditions, agencies collaborate with the Ministry of Social and Family Development to refer vulnerable individuals to the Vulnerable-In-Community Network for assistance. This balanced enforcement approach aims to address root causes while maintaining community sensitivities near temples.

results.title: Partnership Arrangements between Special Education and Mainstream Schools
results.summary: Since 2007, the Ministry of Education has established partnerships between Special Education (SPED) and mainstream schools based on shared outcomes, complementary interests, and geographical proximity. Currently, all SPED schools except two new ones (one operational in 2024, another starting next year) have at least one mainstream partner, with a target of two to three partners per SPED school. The Ministry plans to strengthen these arrangements through sustained student learning experiences and staff professional exchanges, including teacher work attachments, language programmes, and joint community service.

results.title: Percentage of Challenging Questions in 2025 PSLE Main Subject Papers
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that challenging questions constitute approximately 15% of total marks in each of the four main subjects of the 2025 PSLE. All questions, including these challenging items, are based entirely on the primary school syllabus and do not require knowledge beyond the curriculum or private tuition. The Ministry ensures accessibility by using age-appropriate language, authentic contexts, and scaffolding to help students attempt questions progressively. Higher-order thinking skills such as analysis, synthesis, evaluation, and creativity are developed repeatedly throughout the formal education system rather than being taught as specific techniques. Consequently, the PSLE does not inadvertently disadvantage students who cannot afford private tuition, as the assessment of cognitive abilities is integral to the curriculum for all students.

results.title: Possible Integration of Lifestyle Medicine as Preventive Care under Healthier SG
results.summary: The Ministry of Health confirms that lifestyle medicine principles, including nutrition, physical activity, sleep, stress management, and social connection, are already integrated into Healthier SG preventive care. These components guide family doctors via 22 existing Care Protocols and lifestyle prescriptions to help residents manage chronic diseases and adopt healthy habits. Additional protocols will be introduced progressively as part of the ongoing rollout. The Minister noted that these measures are operational and not merely under study.

results.title: Post-graduation Employment Rates of ITE Graduates with Special Educational Needs
results.summary: The Ministry of Education reports that approximately 80% of ITE graduates with Special Educational Needs (SEN) were employed within six months of graduation from 2016 to 2020, excluding the pandemic year 2020, a rate comparable to non-SEN graduates. Among those in full-time permanent roles, roughly 70% held positions directly related to their course of study for both cohorts. To enhance transition outcomes, ITE has expanded the ITE-SG Enable Internship and Employment Support Programme to provide targeted workplace skills training, suitable job placements, and job coaching. The Government will continue monitoring employment outcomes for each graduating cohort and provide further support where necessary.

results.title: Potential Impact of Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone on Competitiveness of Singapore's Maritime and Aviation Hubs
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) will enable Singapore and Johor to complement each other's strengths, allowing companies to leverage Singapore's global networks and connectivity alongside Johor's land and resource advantages. This combined offering is expected to present an attractive value proposition to investors, thereby opening new opportunities that will strengthen the competitiveness of Singapore's maritime and aviation hubs. No specific strategies or timelines for strengthening these hubs were detailed in the response.

results.title: Prevalence of Anxiety and Depressive Symptoms Screened among Women Aged 40 to 59 in Public Primary Care over Past Five Years
results.summary: A 2021 National Healthcare Group study found that 8.7% and 7.3% of patients screened between December 2021 and April 2022 tested positive for clinical depression and anxiety, respectively, with approximately half of these cases involving females. Patients are initially assessed at general clinics before referral to dedicated mental health clinics, where urgent cases receive earlier appointments or specialist referrals. Between 2017 and 2024, the average wait time from initial review to dedicated clinic appointment was 26 days. Specific prevalence data for women aged 40 to 59 over the past five years was not provided in the response.

results.title: Professionalising Skilled Trades
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower defines professionalising skilled trades as adopting professional attributes like structured career pathways, formal training, and certifications to enhance recognition and support these careers. The Ministry is collaborating with stakeholders to develop new initiatives, including structured learning pathways and apprenticeship programmes, with further details to be announced later. Wage growth targets are not currently set; instead, wages will be monitored to ensure they align with service quality, with initiatives calibrated based on impacts to business and consumer costs. Existing regulations already mandate the engagement of licensed tradespersons for specific tasks such as electrical works and complex plumbing, which relevant sector agencies will enforce.

results.title: Programmes to Maintain Employment Stability and Attract MNC Investments amidst Staff Cut Announcements
results.summary: Despite recent staff cut announcements by foreign firms, overall retrenchments remain within historical ranges while PMET resident jobs grew by approximately 35,000 in 2024, driven by growth in financial services, ICT, and professional services. The Government maintains employment stability through a pro-business environment and a skilled workforce, with the Taskforce for Responsible Retrenchment offering career matching and referrals to affected workers. Comprehensive support includes the Career Conversion, SkillsFuture Level-Up, and Mid-Career Pathways Programmes, alongside the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme providing up to $6,000 over six months to eligible lower- and middle-income retrenched individuals seeking roles that better utilize their skills.

results.title: Proportion of Hoarding Cases Monitored by NEAT Resulting in Enforcement Actions
results.summary: Agencies do not track the proportion of New Environment Action Team (NEAT) monitored hoarding cases resulting in enforcement actions, as enforcement is only one of several management measures. NEAT comprises the People's Association, Hoarding Management Core Group agencies, social service providers, community groups, and private organisations. These entities collaborate to identify at-risk cases, provide befriending support to hoarders and families, and address underlying issues such as grief and trauma. Additionally, NEAT works with Town Councils to safely dispose of decluttered materials from Housing and Development Board flats.

results.title: Protocols for Triaging and Referral of Scam Victims with Signs of Acute Psychological Distress
results.summary: Established protocols mandate that scam victims requesting support or assessed as suffering acute psychological distress be referred by Investigation Officers to the Victim Care Cadre Programme. Under this framework, trained Victim Care Officers provide immediate psychological first aid and facilitate referrals to community resources or mental health professionals for longer-term care. The Ministry ensures timely access to these services through direct referral pathways within the programme. No specific timelines or eligibility constraints beyond the assessment of acute distress were stated in the response.

results.title: Providing Affordable Vehicle Access for Families with Special Needs and Elderly Dependants in View of Rising COE Prices
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated the Government will not provide grants via the Certificate of Entitlement (COE) system due to administrative complexity and cost, instead favoring direct subsidies for specific needs. Support for families with special needs dependants, children, and the elderly is channeled through existing schemes like the Enabling Transport Subsidy from the Ministry of Social and Family Development. Additionally, the Government is broadening point-to-point transport options and collaborating with car-sharing operators to secure parking in accessible locations, aiming to improve mobility without altering the COE framework.

results.title: Provision of Alternative Shuttle Bus Services for Commuters Affected by Early Closure of Circle Line Train Services
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that early Circle Line closures are necessary to test Stage 6 opening. Alternative shuttle bus services are not provided after 11 pm because the number of affected commuters is small and sufficient alternative routes exist. The Minister confirmed that shuttle buses are only introduced when the affected commuter volume is significant and no adequate alternative transport options are available. No specific timeline for introducing new services was provided.

results.title: Publicising Removal of Criterion of At Least One Local Employee for Energy Efficiency Grant
results.summary: The Ministry for Trade and Industry confirmed that the removal of the minimum eligibility criterion requiring at least one local employee for the Energy Efficiency Grant (EEG) took effect in June 2025. This policy change was communicated to prospective applicants through relevant materials and the GoBusiness website. The Minister stated that application numbers increased during the three months following the June 2025 implementation. Details regarding the specific communication timeline beyond the website update were not provided in this response.

results.title: Publishing Updated 2015 Occasional Paper on Income Growth, Inequality and Mobility Trends in Singapore
results.summary: The Government plans to update the 2015 Occasional Paper on Income Growth, Inequality, and Mobility Trends in Singapore. Concurrently, efforts are underway to improve data collection on wealth, with findings to be shared upon readiness. The updated paper will provide insights into the state and trends of wealth inequality in Singapore. No specific timeline for the update or the release of wealth data findings has been stated.

results.title: Punitive Actions against Individuals Involved in Sale of Bank Accounts for Scam Purposes
results.summary: On 1 October 2025, a Sri Lankan student was sentenced to three months and two weeks' imprisonment plus a $1,100 fine for relinquishing control of her bank account, which received nearly $18,000 in scam proceeds. Police could not identify the individuals who provided the accused with scammers' contacts. The Minister stated that Police will take tough enforcement action against anyone facilitating scams, including middlemen who recruit money mules. Depending on the facts, such middlemen may face imprisonment of up to 10 years, a fine of up to $500,000, or both.

results.title: Reclassification of Protected Species' Status of Wildlife Animals that Have Caused Repeated Economic Damage to Private Property
results.summary: Protected wildlife status under the Wildlife Act is determined by national conservation status, native origin, and CITES listing, meaning otters remain protected despite causing economic damage. The National Parks Board (NParks) manages human-otter conflicts by assessing risks to public safety and health rather than reclassifying species based solely on property damage. Operational measures include trapping and relocating otters, cordoning off areas near young otters, and using olfactory irritants to guide animals away from residential zones. The Otter Working Group coordinates these efforts among government agencies, experts, and community members to mitigate conflicts without altering the species' protected status.

results.title: Reducing Average Waiting Time for Beds in Nursing Homes
results.summary: The median waiting time for a nursing home bed remains stable at approximately one month, varying by care needs, family preferences, and capacity. The Ministry of Health plans to increase nursing home capacity from over 20,000 beds currently to 31,000 beds by 2030. The Ministry will continue monitoring waiting times and utilisation rates to adjust capacity plans accordingly.

results.title: Regulatory Safeguards for Users of E-commerce Platforms Facilitating Consumer-to-consumer Transactions
results.summary: Carousell and Facebook Marketplace are designated under the Online Criminal Harms Act's E-Commerce Code, legally requiring compliance with user protection measures. Both platforms implemented enhanced verification in 2024, correlating with a 11% scam decrease on Carousell and a 55% decrease on Facebook Marketplace between May and November 2024. The Ministry of Home Affairs continues collaborating with Meta and Carousell on additional anti-scam safeguards and will utilize legal levers where necessary. Consumers are urged to exercise vigilance, particularly on platforms with poor Transaction Safety Ratings, as regulatory measures alone cannot fully eliminate scam risks.

results.title: Regulatory and Deployment Standards for Use of Vape Detection Sensors in Schools and Public Institutions
results.summary: Vape detection sensors are not currently deployed in public institutions, as some schools and Institutes of Higher Learning have assessed them as unsuitable. The Ministry of Health and Health Sciences Authority do not regulate these devices, meaning no approval requirements, technical specifications, data privacy safeguards, or redress mechanisms for false alerts exist. Consequently, no regulatory or deployment standards are published or enforced for trials or permanent rollouts at this time.

results.title: Reliance on A Few Major Cloud Providers and Assessment of Security Risk to Singapore
results.summary: Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) operators must already mitigate over-dependence on major cloud providers through supplier diversity, redundancy, and regular resilience audits under the Cybersecurity Act. The forthcoming Digital Infrastructure Act will strengthen these levers by mandating major cloud service providers to implement security testing, user access controls, proper data governance, and disaster recovery planning aligned with international standards and the February 2025 Advisory Guidelines. While these measures aim to uphold resilience for systemically important digital infrastructure, disruptions cannot be completely prevented. Consequently, businesses are encouraged to conduct risk assessments, diversify service providers, and establish business continuity plans to manage residual risks and reduce impact should a disruption occur.

results.title: Residents' Concerns that Laundry Rack Designs under Home Improvement Programme may be Impractical and Problematic
results.summary: The External Retractable Clothes Drying Rack (ER-CDR) is an essential, fully funded component of the Home Improvement Programme (HIP) for Singapore Citizen households, introduced in 2020 to replace unsafe bamboo poles and address fall risks. The Ministry states the ER-CDR is not optional, as essential HIP improvements are government-funded and installed across the board to ensure safety for an ageing population. HDB has actively engaged residents through demonstrations and feedback collection, resulting in design improvements such as increased laundry capacity and better ergonomics. Recent surveys indicate over 80% of residents consider the rack safe, with close to 70% finding it easy to use after an adjustment period.

results.title: Review of Current Vehicle Licence Classifications for Electric Vehicles
results.summary: The Traffic Police is reviewing vehicle licence classifications to address the higher unladen weight of electric trucks resulting from battery mass. Details regarding whether electric vehicles with an unladen weight up to 3,000kg may be driven under a Class 3 licence instead of Class 4 will be announced shortly. The Ministry has confirmed the review is underway but has not yet released specific outcomes or timelines for the proposed changes.

results.title: Review of Definitions for Public Order Act on Gatherings Outside Istana
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security clarified that organising or participating in a public assembly within the Istana's prohibited area is already an offence under the Public Order Act 2009. This prohibition applies regardless of the number of persons involved, as the Istana and its immediate vicinity are gazetted prohibited areas under the Public Order (Prohibited Areas) Order 2009. Consequently, the Ministry will not review the Act to define gatherings of more than five people as a specific chargeable offence, since the existing law covers all assembly sizes in that zone.

results.title: Review of Policies on Location, Frequency and Design of Designated Smoking Areas within Municipal Boundaries
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed that Designated Smoking Points (DSPs) in municipal areas remain community-led initiatives established where smoking is not prohibited. The National Environment Agency will continue to advise community groups on siting and management to minimize public disamenities. No review of policies regarding the location, frequency, or design of these areas was announced. Consequently, there are no new measures, timelines, or eligibility changes for residents seeking protection from involuntary second-hand smoke exposure under the current framework.

results.title: Review of Public Transport Adequacy and Late-night Transport Services for Attendees of Major Events
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority collaborates with event organisers to extend public transport service hours as needed for large-scale events like the Singapore Grand Prix concerts. Organisers retain responsibility for adhering to agreed dispersal timings and plans to move crowds to public transport nodes. The Ministry will review public transport adequacy for major events to ensure better coordination with organisers and sufficient late-night services for attendees.

results.title: Reviewing School Counsellor Reporting Structures and Providing Training on Counselling Ethics to Enhance Confidentiality and Trust
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) maintains existing reporting structures where school counsellors adhere to ethical standards ensuring student confidentiality, with leaders informed only on a need-to-know basis. Regular consultations occur between counsellors and MOE headquarters for case management, while severe cases are referred to external professionals like the Response, Early intervention and Assessment in Community Mental Health teams. To manage higher caseloads, MOE deploys additional counsellors to ensure manageable workloads and quality care. The Minister confirmed these measures support professional practice and coordinated student support without detailing specific future reviews of reporting structures or new training programmes for principals.

results.title: Reviews and Audits of Course Listings on SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme Portal
results.summary: SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) regularly reviews and audits funded courses on the SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme portal, removing those that are discontinued or deemed not industry-relevant. While some listings lack scheduled runs due to provider-dependent demand thresholds, the portal now includes filters for availability. SSG collaborates with providers to launch new programmes in high-demand sectors like artificial intelligence and digital analytics, with over 100 such courses introduced this year to align offerings with hiring needs.

results.title: Safeguarding Sensitive Information when Sharing Classified Threat Intelligence with Critical Sector Organisations
results.summary: The Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA) will share classified threat intelligence with Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) owners to help detect specific threats, implementing safeguards such as redacting sensitive national security information and requiring compliance with the Official Secrets Act. CSA continues to update the Cybersecurity Code of Practice standards and provides tools to assist CII owners in meeting their regulatory obligations under the Cybersecurity Act, while CII owners remain ultimately accountable for their systems' cybersecurity outcomes. To strengthen the national cybersecurity workforce, CSA partners with industry and academic entities to train professionals, increasing Singapore's cybersecurity workforce from approximately 5,000 eight years ago to about 10,000 today. Further updates on progress to strengthen Singapore's cybersecurity core will be provided in response to related oral questions scheduled for a subsequent sitting.

results.title: Safeguards to Ensure Singapore Family Offices are Not Misused to Facilitate Money Laundering
results.summary: The Minister for Finance deferred the response regarding safeguards against family office misuse for money laundering to the next Sitting on 5 November 2025. This delay applies to the current question and similar Parliamentary Questions submitted for consideration. No specific measures, timelines, or operational constraints were announced during this session. The Government has not yet stated the steps it will take to address potential misuse of family offices at this time.

results.title: School Meal Safety Standards and Continuity Measures with Implementation of Centralised Kitchen Model
results.summary: From January 2026, central kitchen operators must comply with Singapore Food Agency (SFA) requirements, including certified food handlers, temperature-controlled logistics, accurate time-stamped labelling, and appointed Food Hygiene Officers. SFA employs a risk-based inspection approach with stepped-up surveillance for high-volume central kitchens. Operators must maintain comprehensive contingency plans for supply disruptions, featuring rapid notification protocols and backup meal arrangements via other licensed kitchens. Schools will reinforce personal hygiene reminders, while MOE monitors student health closely, promptly notifying the Ministry of Health and SFA of any potential food poisoning incidents.

results.title: Singapore Sign Language as Elective for Secondary School Students
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that Singapore Sign Language (SgSL) is currently used in designated mainstream schools like Mayflower Primary and Beatty Secondary to support deaf and hard-of-hearing students. These schools provide resources and trained staff for inclusive learning, while hearing students at Beatty Secondary can learn SgSL to communicate with peers. For students in other schools interested in learning SgSL, the Minister directed them to approach providers such as the Singapore Association for the Deaf. The Ministry has not indicated plans to offer SgSL as a general elective for all secondary students.

results.title: Singapore's Money Laundering Investigations into Cambodia-based Network Designated as Transnational Criminal Organisation
results.summary: The Minister for Finance deferred answering questions regarding MAS investigations into a Cambodia-based transnational criminal organisation designated by the US in October 2025. The response, covering inquiries into pre-designation investigations of affiliated individuals and Singapore entities, will be provided at the next Sitting on 5 November 2025. No specific findings, timelines, or operational constraints were disclosed in the current transcript.

results.title: Singapore's Stance on Maritime Jurisdiction in Light of Interception of Global Sumud Flotilla
results.summary: Singapore's position regarding the interception of the Global Sumud Flotilla emphasizes adherence to international law, including the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS). The Minister stated that rules governing maritime areas affected by conflict apply equally within the Gaza Strip. Singapore calls on all parties in the Gaza conflict to comply with relevant international humanitarian law and the law of the sea. The matter of the interception's legality is to be settled at an appropriate forum. Singapore notes that respect for international law is critical for small island states like itself.

results.title: Slowdown on Hiring of New Nursing Degree Graduates and/or Early-Career Nurses at Restructured Hospitals
results.summary: Minister Ong Ye Kung stated that hiring of local nurses in public healthcare clusters in 2025 remains comparable to previous years despite system expansion. While demand is strong, some applicants prefer specific work locations or shift patterns that do not align with institutional needs. The Ministry encourages graduates to consider a wider variety of work conditions to gain richer experience. No reduction in hiring or placement of new nursing degree or diploma holders has been reported.

results.title: Staff to Child Ratio in Preschools and Student Care Centres
results.summary: The Ministry for Social and Family Development mandates minimum staff-to-child ratios for preschools and Student Care Fee Assistance centres to ensure child safety. Preschool ratios range from one qualified staff per five infants to one per 25 children at the kindergarten 2 level. Similarly, registered student care centres must maintain a ratio of at least one staff to 25 students. Centres retain operational flexibility to deploy additional staff beyond these minimums based on their programming objectives and available manpower.

results.title: Standardising and Capping Medical Report Fees for Activation of LPA across Different Hospitals to Prevent Disparities
results.summary: Public healthcare institutions charge variable medical report fees for Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) activation on a cost recovery basis, reflecting differing manpower and administrative costs for complex cases. The Ministry does not plan to standardise or cap these fees but directs patients with financial difficulties to seek assistance from medical social workers at public healthcare institutions. No means-tested subsidies or specific financial assistance schemes for LPA activation were proposed in the response.

results.title: Standards to Deter Child Trafficking in Cross-border Adoptions in ASEAN
results.summary: Singapore maintains robust legal frameworks to deter child trafficking in cross-border adoptions, citing the Adoption of Children Act, Penal Code, and the 2015 ASEAN Convention Against Trafficking in Persons. The Minister highlighted existing regional cooperation under the ASEAN Regional Plan of Action (2016–2025), which establishes extraterritorial jurisdiction for trafficking offences and mandates a "4Ps" approach covering prevention, prosecution, protection, and partnership. Domestic safeguards include strict verification of identity papers, travel documents, and birth parent consent to prevent unlawful transfers for exploitation or financial gain. While the Government asserts these measures effectively combat trafficking alongside ASEAN partners, the transcript does not specify new steps taken beyond these established laws and frameworks.

results.title: Statistics for Tours, Educational Visits and Public Engagement Activities at Local Farms
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) website provides a non-exhaustive list of local land-based farms offering public tours. Visits to sea-based farms are not encouraged due to public safety and biosecurity constraints. SFA focuses on supporting local farm growth and productivity through technology adoption rather than specific incentives for public engagement. Instead, SFA collaborates with the Ministry of Education to raise community awareness of local produce benefits via school educational initiatives on food security. No enhanced steps or financial incentives for farms to offer tours were explicitly stated.

results.title: Statistics on Mobile Dental Clinics Serving Schools without Dental Clinics
results.summary: The School Dental Service deploys 30 mobile dental clinics to serve secondary and Special Education schools lacking static facilities. These clinics visit each designated school once annually, eliminating the need for booking or waiting periods. Most primary schools already possess on-site dental clinics and do not require mobile services. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health confirmed these operational details regarding the current deployment and scheduling of the mobile units.

results.title: Statutory Study and Training Leave Policies in Other Countries that May Be Feasible for Singapore
results.summary: The Ministry regularly reviews international statutory training leave models but does not mandate such leave in Singapore. Instead, it maintains a flexible system supporting lifelong learning through SkillsFuture Singapore and Workforce Singapore initiatives like Absentee Payroll funding and Training Allowance. The Government encourages flexible work arrangements to balance training with employment. It will continue monitoring global best practices to ensure workforce competitiveness and training accessibility without introducing specific statutory leave entitlements.

results.title: Strategies to Support SMEs Participation in GRaduate Industry Traineeships Programme
results.summary: Workforce Singapore prioritizes host organizations' ability to deliver quality training when selecting participants for the GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) Programme. While SMEs lacking economies of scale or training infrastructure can participate, they must meet specific programme requirements. The Ministry collaborates with sector agencies to curate eligible host organizations based on their track record of hiring and training locals. Consequently, SMEs without the necessary infrastructure may not be selected unless they demonstrate compliance with these core criteria.

results.title: Strengthening Engineering Talent Pipelines through Aligning with Emerging Industries and Public Sector Hiring
results.summary: The Ministry of Education aligns Institutes of Higher Learning (IHL) engineering supply with industry demand, noting that nearly 30% of recent graduates studied engineering. While interest in Built Environment sectors has declined due to talent shortages and salary constraints, the Taskforce of Architectural and Engineering Consultants recommends extending outreach, enhancing internships, and offering higher starting salaries to improve attraction and retention. To bolster the talent pipeline, the Public Service increased its Science, Technology and Engineering (STE) officer recruitment by 3.4% between 2019 and 2024, distributing 50 to 60 undergraduate scholarships annually alongside the Public Service Commission Scholarship (Engineering). Additionally, MOE provides approximately 200 pre-university scholarships yearly to students with strong mathematics and science foundations to encourage STEM careers.

results.title: Students Progressing from Special Education School into ITE Progammes
results.summary: In the last five years, approximately 480 Special Education (SPED) graduates have been recorded annually, with around 90 accessing the national curriculum. Of these, roughly 70 students per year, representing about 80% of the national curriculum group, completed GCE "N" or "O" levels and progressed to institutions like the Institute of Technical Education (ITE) or polytechnics. Additionally, a small cohort of about 10 graduates annually with higher support needs who complete vocational certification in SPED also transition to ITE. The Ministry plans to facilitate further transitions for students with diverse needs through the MOE-SG Enable School-to-Work Transition Programme, focusing on direct employment pathways rather than further academic progression.

results.title: Study on Lottery Gambling Addiction and Review of Policy of Not Requiring Identification for Lottery Purchases at Singapore Pools Physical Outlets
results.summary: The Ministry reports that lottery gambling addiction rates in Singapore have remained low and stable at approximately 1% of the resident population aged 18 and above, based on surveys conducted in 2017, 2020, and 2023. Consequently, the current policy does not require identification for lottery purchases at Singapore Pools physical outlets, as these are deemed lower risk compared to casinos, gaming machine rooms, or remote gambling where identification is mandatory. The exclusion regime, which applies to riskier gambling products, is not extended to physical lottery outlets because they pose a lower risk profile. The Ministry maintains this balance to allow limited gambling while ensuring problem gambling remains under control through existing social safeguards.

results.title: Support for Children Diagnosed with Rare Diseases that Require High-cost or Long-term Treatment
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track the specific number of children diagnosed with rare diseases. Clinically and cost-effective treatments, including those for rare diseases, are subsidised at public healthcare institutions, with MediSave and MediShield Life covering selected high-cost drugs and cell, tissue, and gene therapies. The Rare Disease Fund provides additional support for treatments not covered by subsidies or insurance, featuring a 3:1 Government matching rate for donations. Beyond medical costs, patients with disabilities can access long-term care subsidies and grants, which will be progressively enhanced from 2026, alongside monthly payouts of up to $400 from the Home Caregiving Grant for community-based caregiving. Supportive equipment costs are offset via the Assistive Technology Fund, with financial difficulties addressed through medical social workers and MediFund.

results.title: Support for Smaller Firms to Achieve Workplace Safety and Health Accreditation
results.summary: As of September 2025, the Workplace Safety and Health Advocate Programme has benefited nearly 2,900 SMEs through 37 advocates who integrate safety records into contract awards. The Government supports SMEs achieving bizSAFE accreditation via a multi-pronged approach: StartSAFE offers fully subsidised risk management training and processes; grants like the Productivity Solutions Grant fund WSH technology adoption; and the Enhanced Training Support for SMEs provides higher subsidies for training. Additionally, the WSH Council partners with SME Centres to guide capability enhancement. These measures collectively address resource constraints by covering training costs, funding technology solutions, and facilitating independent verification to help smaller firms attain WSH accreditation.

results.title: Tackling Food Safety Incidence at Schools and Institutes of Higher Learning
results.summary: School and IHL canteen stalls must comply with SFA hygiene protocols, with licensees requiring certifications and maintaining premises cleanliness. Premises with past infringements face stepped-up surveillance. Recent food safety incidents remain isolated and contained. Upon occurrence, swift interventions include medical support for affected students, notifying SFA and the Ministry of Health for investigations, and engaging parents to monitor children's health. The Minister stated these measures ensure strict adherence to food safety standards and effective containment of any incidents.

results.title: Tax Incentives Received by Singapore Family Office Associated with Individuals Sanctioned by US in October 2025
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance deferred answering the question regarding tax incentives received by a local single family office associated with US-sanctioned individuals until the next Sitting on 5 November 2025. This delay applies to the inquiry about the tax incentive scheme for family offices alongside similar Parliamentary Questions. No specific details on the incentives, amounts, or eligibility conditions were provided in the current transcript. The response will be delivered at the specified future date.

results.title: Strengthening the Mental Resilience of Our Young
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) responded to a proposal for a School Mental Well-being Charter by stating it does not currently utilize a Charter-based approach but instead relies on systemic processes to foster resilience. MOE clarified that resilience is a trainable skill involving confidence and relationship abilities rather than the mere absence of mental illness or the removal of all stressors. While the proposed Charter would be co-led by MOE, the Health Promotion Board, and the Institute of Mental Health to set developmental milestones, MOE will study the proposal's feasibility. Existing frameworks include the CCE curriculum guided by developmental milestones for primary to tertiary levels, termly student well-being check-in surveys, and insights from recent studies such as the Youth Epidemiology and Resilience Study. The ministry emphasized that a systematic, sustained national approach is required beyond individual school initiatives to build psychological immunity before crises occur.

results.title: Timeline Updates on Roll-out Plan for Active Ageing Centres within Private Estates
results.summary: Active Ageing Centres (AACs) typically require approximately two years to establish due to site identification, provider appointment, construction, and operational setup. Securing suitable sites in private estates remains challenging. The Ministry of Health aims to expand AAC services in private estates over the next three years to reach an additional 20,000 seniors. The Ministry will continue collaborating with agencies and Grassroots Advisors to explore methods for extending these services, while welcoming suggestions for suitable spaces.

results.title: Tracking Knife Crimes Involving Offenders with Mental Health Issues
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs does not track knife crime incidents specifically involving offenders with mental health issues. Instead, it regulates the sale of restricted knives like flick knives under the Guns, Explosives and Weapons Control Act 2021 and imposes strict penalties for carrying offensive weapons in public. When knife crime incidents occur, the Police neutralize threats and arrest offenders, subsequently referring those with mental health issues to medical practitioners for treatment or to Community Outreach Teams (CREST) and the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC). These agencies then refer cases to appropriate mental health service providers to address underlying issues.

results.title: Tracking of Annual Malnutrition Rates Among Seniors for Past Five Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Health has already addressed this query via an oral reply to Question No 43 on the Order Paper dated 4 November 2025. Consequently, the specific annual malnutrition rates among seniors for the past five years are not provided in this written response. Readers seeking the requested statistics should refer to the official record of that oral reply for the relevant data.

results.title: Unfair Contractual Terms in Career Conversion Programmes that Require Trainees to Pay Compensation for Termination
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that companies engaging in egregious practices, such as recovering costs exceeding actual training expenditure or including government subsidies in liquidated damages calculations, will be suspended from the Career Conversion Programme (CCP). While legitimate termination for misconduct may incur liquidated damages, employers must not seek refunds exceeding their net actual costs after deducting subsidies. Trainees facing unreasonable clauses can seek assistance from unions, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices, or the Ministry of Manpower. The Government is committed to safeguarding trainee interests, though specific new guidelines on prohibited terms were not confirmed in this response.

results.title: Update on Jobs Transformation Maps and Support Available for Mid-Career Employees and Sectors Undergoing Restructuring
results.summary: Nineteen Jobs Transformation Maps (JTMs) have been launched, covering approximately 1.7 million employed residents to guide workforce interventions. Workforce Singapore utilizes JTMs to shape sectoral initiatives, including Job Redesign and Reskilling Career Conversion Programmes that reskill workers into growth roles identified in the maps. In 2024, these programmes enabled over 400 companies to reskill workers into growth roles, benefiting around 2,300 individuals. For sectors undergoing restructuring, JTMs assist agencies like the Monetary Authority of Singapore and the Singapore Tourism Board in anticipating future needs and developing targeted upskilling interventions based on specific industry insights.

results.title: Update on Police Efforts to Tackle Bullying and Assault Incidents Outside Temples along Waterloo Mall
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force (SPF) conducts regular patrols along Waterloo Street, particularly during religious festivals, and takes action against bullying or assault incidents. The SPF collaborates with the Singapore Land Authority, Singapore Food Agency, Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth, and Ministry of Social and Family Development to maintain order. While the SPF adopts a measured approach for cases involving vulnerable individuals without outright crimes, public disorder is not condoned, and investigations follow any committed crimes. The Ministry emphasizes working closely with sister agencies to address the complex dimensions of incidents involving vulnerable people in the area.

results.title: Update on Review of Direct School Admission Scheme
results.summary: The review of the Direct School Admission (DSA) scheme remains ongoing as a Ministry priority. Minister Desmond Lee confirmed that further details regarding the review's status and outcomes will be shared when ready. No specific timeline for the release of results or reasons for any potential de-prioritisation was provided in the response. The Ministry has not yet indicated if the review has been de-prioritised, leaving the 2025 priority status and exact completion date unconfirmed at this time.

results.title: Update on whether Two-day Work-from-home Arrangement Remains as Default Policy in Civil Service
results.summary: The two-day work-from-home arrangement is not the default policy for all Civil Service officers, as approximately half require physical presence for roles like frontline service, security, or teaching. Flexible work arrangements remain available for eligible officers, including part-time work or staggered hours, but telecommuting is not an entitlement. Operational needs take priority over individual preferences, and officers must return to the office when duties require it. The government aims to support officers' needs while maintaining Public Service operational effectiveness without compromising service delivery.

results.title: Updating Organ Donation Scheme by Strengthening Legislation and Consolidating Donations under National Registry
results.summary: The Minister stated that existing provisions under the Human Organ Transplant Act and Medical (Therapy, Education and Research) Act adequately safeguard donor wishes, with healthcare teams addressing family objections through established protocols. The Ministry of Health confirmed no plans to consolidate organ, body, and brain donations into a single registry, noting that Singaporeans and Permanent Residents can already register decisions via the national Organ Donor Registry portal. Brain donations remain managed separately at Brain Bank Singapore for individuals ineligible under the Medical (Therapy, Education and Research) Act, while separate processes ensure all registered decisions are honoured upon death.

results.title: Upgrading Costs and Plans for Eunos Community Club
results.summary: The People's Association scaled down upgrading works for the Eunos Community Club to an essential scope costing approximately $3.1 million after nine evaluated bids exceeded the estimated value, leading to the decision not to proceed with the full tender. These essential works, including mechanical and electrical upgrades like the lift system, are currently underway with a target reopening in early 2026. Future upgrading needs will be assessed progressively in consultation with residents and stakeholders after the current project concludes. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that the tender was originally called in September 2022, with two bids disqualified by the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore prior to evaluation.

results.title: Usage Statistics for Free Marine Parade Shuttle Bus Service and Cost Assessment of Programme
results.summary: The Marine Parade shuttle bus pilot, launched to test last-mile connectivity via Grassroots Organisations, recorded variable usage across routes and times. The initial estimated cost of $1 million remains accurate, with funding primarily sourced from GRO donations and a $200,000 seed grant from the South East Community Development Council. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed these figures, noting that specific passenger load statistics were not provided as usage fluctuated significantly by location and period. No further details on total publicly funded amounts beyond the seed grant were disclosed in the response.

results.title: Utilisation Pattern of Child Development Accounts over Last Five Years During Children's Primary School Years
results.summary: For birth cohorts turning 12 between 2020 and 2024, the median Child Development Account (CDA) balance at age seven was approximately $600. About 97% of CDA expenditure occurred before age seven, primarily for preschool expenses, while funds between ages seven and 12 were mostly used for healthcare or siblings' preschool costs. For primary school learning needs, parents can utilize the Edusave account for miscellaneous fees and approved enrichment programmes instead of drawing on CDA funds. Unused CDA monies are transferred to the Post-Secondary Education Account at age 12 for post-secondary expenses. The Government will continue reviewing CDA usage to ensure relevance and meet parents' needs for children's development.

results.title: Utilisation Rate of SG60 Credit Top-Up for ActiveSG Users
results.summary: The SG60 ActiveSG Credit Top-Up provides $100 credits to all Singaporeans and PRs who log in to MyActiveSG+ between 2 June 2025 and 31 December 2025. These credits are valid until end-2026, automatically roll over if at least one transaction is made, and cover booking fees, gym/pool passes, and programme fees. As of 30 September 2025, over 1.1 million members claimed credits, with approximately 41% making at least one transaction. Sport Singapore will continue expanding offerings, including pickleball, street dance, and workshops on muscle fitness, balance, and nutrition to encourage active lifestyles.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of SkillsFuture Credits to Offset Tuition Fees at Autonomous Universities over Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, approximately one in three Singaporeans pursuing postgraduate by coursework full qualification programmes at autonomous universities received Ministry of Education (MOE) subsidies, while another one in three utilised SkillsFuture Credits to offset tuition fees regardless of subsidy status. Eligibility for MOE postgraduate subsidies remains available to individuals who previously received Government subsidies or sponsorship for their undergraduate degrees. The Minister clarified that receiving undergraduate support does not disqualify applicants from future postgraduate subsidies.

results.title: Workplace Fairness (Dispute Resolution) Bill
results.summary: The Workplace Fairness (Dispute Resolution) Bill establishes a new statutory framework effective end-2027, mandating a three-step dispute resolution process: internal grievance handling, mandatory mediation via the Tripartite Alliance for Fair Employment Practices (TAFEP), and adjudication at the Employment Claims Tribunals (ECT) or High Court as a last resort. While the legislation strengthens protections against discrimination and complements existing Tripartite Guidelines, parliamentary debate highlighted concerns regarding accessibility for vulnerable groups, including non-unionised workers, persons with disabilities, and those with limited English proficiency. Specific questions were raised about the flexibility of time bars for filing claims, the adequacy of support mechanisms for SMEs, and the potential for frivolous claims to burden employers. The Minister affirmed the Bill's intent to balance justice with workplace harmony, noting that implementation efforts will continue to ensure employers understand their duties and workers know their rights upon the Act's commencement.
